missables: Al Bhed Primer Vol. XII — platform above O'aka at the North Wharf; collect before leaving the wharf
Al Bhed Primer Vol. XIII — room west of the red doors in Guadosalam; collect before story advances
Hi-Potion ×2 — upper balcony of Seymour's mansion; collect before entering the purple doors
Lightning Marble ×8 — corridor to the Farplane; check left wall immediately upon entering
sgTip: Rikku joins with an open Sphere Grid path — if you have spare Ability Spheres and unused nodes nearby, activate as many as you can before leaving Guadosalam. Her Mix Overdrive also scales with the number of items you carry, so stock up on common consumables at the shops here.

subLocations.name: North Wharf
subLocations.prose: Rikku rejoins the party at the North Wharf after being pulled from the shoopuf. Two quick tutorials trigger here: one teaching how Rikku can steal from treasure chests during combat, the second introducing her Mix Overdrive. Use the Save Sphere before heading out. O'aka is at his stall but only selling medicines — still worth stocking up given Customize becomes available shortly. Before you follow the path west, climb the platform above O'aka to grab Primer XII. You cannot return to the North Wharf once you leave. Grab the Ether from the chest on the trail heading west, then talk to the Guado you pass on the road to Guadosalam.

subLocations.name: Road to Guadosalam
subLocations.prose: Two chests line the short trail into town: Antidote x4 in the first chest on the right and a Mega-Potion on the left. Don't sell the Antidotes — they are prime Customize fodder. At this point in the game, Antidotes, Eye Drops, and Echo Screens can all add useful abilities to empty weapon and armor slots once Rikku's Customize tutorial unlocks inside Guadosalam. You will also pass several Guado on the path; none are threats, but it is worth speaking to them for flavour before the story picks up at the town gates.

subLocations.name: Guadosalam
subLocations.prose: Mostly story — Seymour's feast, the Farplane, and the proposal. Explore the winding backstreets before entering the red double doors; there are chests with a Mega-Potion, Elixir, and 3,000 gil tucked around the western room, plus Primer XIII on the floor. In the corridor leading to the Farplane, check the left wall for a hidden chest with Lightning Marbles. On the Farplane itself, speak to Wakka and Lulu to trigger their scenes — this upgrades the Brotherhood with Strength +10%, Sensor, and Waterstrike. Notice that everyone except Tidus sees a loved one — his father's spirit appears to him instead, visually changed in a way that quietly confirms what Jecht has become. You already know, but it's the game's first explicit on-screen acknowledgement.

subLocations.name: Seymour's Mansion
subLocations.prose: Enter through the large red double doors. Before heading to the feast, ascend either staircase to the upper balcony — there is a hidden chest there with two Hi-Potions that you cannot access after entering the purple doors. Speak to all party members scattered around the main hall, then push through the purple double doors to trigger Seymour's feast scene. After the feast and Seymour's proposal to Yuna, the scene ends and you return outside. Rikku receives a Customize tutorial from Tromell at this point — if you have excess Antidotes and other medicines from the road, this is the moment to start adding abilities to empty gear slots.

subLocations.name: Corridor to the Farplane
subLocations.prose: As soon as you enter the corridor leading to the Farplane, move left along the left-hand wall before following the party up the stairs. There is a hidden chest here with eight Lightning Marbles, and it is trivially easy to walk straight past if you follow the automatic camera angle up toward the stairs. Lightning Marbles are a valuable Customize ingredient for adding Lightning-elemental properties to weapons; pick them up even if you don't plan to Customize immediately. The Venus Crest (Lulu's Onion Knight celestial component) is also obtainable in this corridor during the second visit to Guadosalam — note it for later.
